zotero.abstractNote: Approximate Bayesian inference methods are ubiquitous in machine learning. However, they often introduce implicit inductive biases that are difficult to characterize. In this paper, we propose a diagnostic tool to discover the inductive bias of an approximate inference method. We frame the approximate posterior as the exact posterior of a Gibbs prior, which is a perturbation of the original prior. By analyzing this Gibbs prior, we can interpret the inductive bias of the approximation as a modification of the prior. We demonstrate our approach on several examples, including Gaussian process regression and multi-task learning, and show that it provides insights into the behavior of approximate inference methods.
